Notes / Comments / Special Instructions / Restrictions*BES said to sell if this is at a loss, I went through a whole cost basis issue with amy stolarski last year. this is FID info on the cost basis from when all the Stolarski's inherited these shares- "On an average cost position, an average is calculated for both the covered and non-covered lots. In other words, there are two average costs. The date of death was 11/22/2024. The step-up price was $12.14 per share. However, the 12.14 per share was only applied to the non-covered tax lots due to the fact this is an average cost position. The covered tax lots show a cost basis of 11.82 per share. This is because there were purchases after the date of death at a lower price that are included in the average cost calculation. Disqualifying events (sales) prevent the client from switching this position from average cost to actual cost. Also, please note the original cost basis on file is third party basis that was sent to us from a contra firm." so either way this is at a loss so sell, but I wanted to provide the basis info I got last year and it should be avail

tasks.notes:
Forms Requiring SupervisionThe list below shows the forms that require supervisory review by a supervisor or designee. To ensure proper supervision, please review the list and select the appropriate supervision path (“Requires Supervision” or “Does not require Supervision”) when submitting additional paperwork in CLIC® for an existing account. Note that if any form in the batch is included on the list, the entire batch requires supervision. Any form that requires a principal or supervisor signature must also be submitted through a supervised trade key.

Forms requiring supervision generally fall into these categories:

Suitability Forms
All versions of Cambridge’s Client Information and Suitability forms, including Trust/Entity, Group, Additional Signers, etc.
Resident Alien Questionnaire, Politically Exposed Person Questionnaire, and US Resident Client Living Abroad Questionnaire
Applications and account opening documents
Fund company applications (annuity, alternative investments, mutual funds, etc.), custodian account applications, Third Party Manager applications, checkwriting and margin applications, change of broker-dealer forms, record keeper service agreements, etc.
Cambridge product disclosures and suitability rationale documents
Annuity Disclosure, Alternative Investment Disclosure, 529 Plan Disclosure, etc.
Best Interest Analysis forms, RightBRIDGE Annuity Wizard, and RightBRIDGE Product Profiler
Investment Exchange Disclosures
Management Agreements, including fee arrangements
Financial Planning and Consulting documents
Legal Documents and Authorized Signer forms
Documents that indicate who is authorized to act on investment accounts, such as Corporate Resolution forms, Trustee Certification of Investment Powers, Power of Attorney documents, etc.
Account maintenance and supplemental forms
Margin agreements, options agreements, check writing applications
For directly held accounts, sponsor maintenance forms requiring supervision include those with financial or control implications—such as beneficiary updates, withdrawal requests to third parties, addition of trading authority, and any forms requiring a Medallion Signature Guarantee
While Cambridge does not require supervision of routine maintenance forms (e.g., address changes or standard first party banking information) we do require that they be maintained in the client file for books and records purposes. Additionally, the sponsor company may have specific submission and approval policies that need to be followed. It is important to note that address changes direct at sponsors do not typically carry over to Cambridge systems.

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE TEMPLATES
tasks.notes:
**✅** ** Adding Templates toRightBRIDGE**

**BeforeYou Begin:**

There are **16 models** to add

Template IDs: **1489–1504**

* * *

**Steps:**

1. **Log     into CIR2.**
2. On
     the left-hand side, **click “RightBRIDGE.”**
3. Under
     **Investment Wizard Tools**, select **“Template Management.”**
4. Click
     **“Add Shared Template.”**
5. Enter
     the first **Template ID** (starting with 1489).
6. Click
     **“Save Template.”**
    - ⚠️ Important: You
      must hit **Save** before moving to the next template.
7. Repeat
     steps **4–6** for each ID through **1504.**
8. After
     all templates are added, confirm that **all 16 templates** appear in
     the list.

* * *

**OngoingMaintenance:**

- This
     is a **manual process.**
- When
     model changes occur, **Beamer will update** the RightBRIDGE templates.
- You
     will receive a **new shared template number** that must be added
     following the same process above.
- **For     future updates:**
     Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.
- It
     goes quickly, and this ensures **nothing is missed** or left outdated.

* * *

💬 **Need help?**

I’m happy to walk through these steps if needed — and for remote team members,
we can screen share to go through it together!

**Forfuture updates:**
Delete **all existing templates** first, then re-add all new ones.  It
goes quickly, and this ensures nothing is missed or left outdated.

tasks.title: RIGHTBRIDGE
tasks.notes:
**LASTUPDATED: 1/15/24 (zwb)** 

**It isimportant to note that the Rightbridge (RB) platform is onlyavailable to licensed admins/advisors. If you are not licensed you will beunable to complete this process** 

**Rightbridge isalso semi-frequently updated slightly so things may look different then theimages posted below** 

**Zoom in ifunable to read the images due to bluriness** 

1. First, login to https://www.cir2.com/ and
     navigate to the RB tool in the tool bar
2. Once you have entered the RB
     platform you will be presented with 4 options. We are currently not
     concerned with the Annuity or Investment Wizard's so dis regard these two
     options.

1. From this page you can select
     previous cases so you can edit them or copy them, but we are interested in
     the "Create New Case" tab under the product profiler.
2. This will launch the client
     information tab requiring information found on either RT, CLIC, or in the
     RB template created for you. If the client is married the spouses info
     will be required as well.
3. Next, the system will request a
     product to be validated which includes investment objectives, time
     horizon, product (advisory/commission account), amount, and fee. Check the
     RB template as well as CLIC for this information.
4. We then need to select the
     account(s) that we are transferring from, which will create additional
     pages for each account. The 3 I have selected are the most common we use
     when transferring to a traditional IRA. We also use "Cash/Current
     Income" for new ROTH IRAs frequently, but this does not require
     additional info besides the amount.
    1. 401k:
        1. If you
       know the name of the 401k plan, you can search for it to input known
       information, otherwise fill the account fees to the best of your
       ability. Typically, we don't have access to participant fees.
        2. As for
       the account features, below is a general template to follow as this
       applies to most people that want to transfer their money to us. However,
       adjust as needed.
    2. Advisory
      IRA:
        1. For
       advisory accounts, look at the statements for the account that is
       transferring for fee information. While the fee % most likely won't be
       listed, we can find the amount pulled and do the math to determine the
       fee %. (If you are unsure you can discuss with the advisor)
        2. For the
       fund fee, you can guesstimate the average if there are a large amount of
       mutual funds, or lookup the funds to calculate the amount. If it is all
       stocks then the fund fee is 0%.
        3. For
       account feature, adjust as needed.
    3. Brokerage
      IRA: 
        1. Brokerage
       IRAs have the same account features as Advisory, but the fee questions
       are different. Update as needed based off the statements or info from
       advisor.

1. The financial details page only
     has NIA currently which should be listed in the template or CLIC
2. Client preferences is a case by
     case basis, although you can find some of this info in CLIC and the
     following images are the typical responses for our clients. Discuss with
     the advisor if you have questions.
    1. Risk tolerance should be available
      in CLIC, liquidity is the focus unless we are doing an annuity, and for
      advice we put someone else managing it a majority of the time.
    2. Adjust fee sensitivity if you find
      it relevant, and insurance need should be "The client does not
      desire a product with death benefits" unless we are doing an annuity
      or insurance product. This RB is only focused on the one account, so it
      will give you an error if you want death benefits with an advisory
      account because you are unable to meet this need.
    3. For form CRS delivery date put the
      day the ppwk was signed or is going to be signed.
3. On the "Results" page,
     you may be required to provide additional notes based on which product(s)
     you are moving from, the new account you are opening, and how the
     questions were answered.
    1. Below is a typical error that
      shows up along with the usual response when a new client is switching
      advisors.

“Please provide additional rationale for
the selected recommendation” – Not happy with current advisor/investment
options

    1. Below
      is an example of a more in depth error that may occur, the test client is
      55 and thus the info is relevant to their situation. Discuss with advisor
      if you need clarification for the notes.

“Please
provide additional rationale for the selected recommendation” – Client has
additional outside accounts to pull from, and is not concerned with potential penalties
and would rather have broader investment options.

1. Once 
     you have completed all required notes, Click on the case summary button on
     the left of the screen.
2. Click the dropdown arrow for
     "Product Profiler" and select "Preview Product Profiler
     PDF" to download the RB. Provide to whoever needs the RB and copy to
     the template used to request to a RT note.

**A fewkey reminders:**

- For **advisory     accounts**, since we use **Nick Raiche and Horizons** in our model
     management selection, we should be selecting **Option 3 or 4** for
     objectives & preferences.

Prefers a broad mix of investment with oversight form an external
fund manager or team of managers that can actively make portfolio changes

Prefers a higher level of oversight beyond fund managers, including
fund screening and control over fund reallocation.

- For **commission-based,     directly held accounts** (mostly buy & hold), RightBRIDGE should
     reflect that. Please be sure to choose **one of the two circled options**
     for those.

The client would like to make their own decisions

The client will make their own decision, but would like
suggestions

-

tasks.title: Moline QCD
tasks.notes:
Hi Don, here is some Qualified Charitable Distribution info.  If you want to do this in 2026 we should do it in January or February before your RMD.  We will need the non profit name, amount of donation, tax id number, and address to send it to.  We can send you the form to sign electronically and then send out your donations. https://www.fidelity.com/retirement-ira/required-minimum-distributions-qcds

https://www.irs.gov/retirement-plans/retirement-plans-faqs-regarding-iras-distributions-withdrawals

I’ll follow up next week on the allocation changes we discussed.

Happy Thanksgiving!  Great seeing you and Beth.

Once funds become available, we will proceed with maximizing contributions to your Schwab Roth IRA. Could you please confirm if those funds are ready to be pulled into the Schwab Roth IRA account? After that, we will send you the necessary paperwork to establish and transfer assets into a Fidelity Roth IRA. Additionally, we will be sending a Transfer of Assets form to move your existing Fidelity IRA held outside of SDC into your Fidelity IRA with SDC. If you need any assistance or have any questions, please feel free to reach out to Jaiden or Brent! 04/02/2026 - sent Todd the following MRC message and resent the Docusign (baw) ​​​​​​Good Morning Todd! If you have any questions or need anything from me, I’m more than happy to help. Thank you so much, and I appreciate your time! 03/13/26- Sent out checkwriting forms to Tom, I copied Carolyn Berryman's checkwriting form. Sent Tom the following MRC: Hi Tom, I just sent over another DocuSign for you. Type of Paperwork Being Pulled: Existing Account Maintenance

FID TRAD IRA 636-302468, TRAD IRA   628-426222, TRAD IRA 628-426216, TRAD IRA 628-426212, ROTH IRA    226-497172, RIVER ROAD LLC #  636-572536,  TO DO Legacy 636857784 (LLC), TO DO legacy 36857808 (LLC) Retirement \

 02/17/26- Got with Marv bc I didn't see Donna listed anywhere in the trust saved in misc folders, we dug through notes and couldn't find anything. I called KSO and she said that Donna is not listed as a trustee anywhere and to push both activities back until JEK & BES are back to clarify what is going on. (ctb)

02/20/26- Tom got hacked and they shut down all of his FID accounts, pushing this off for 2 weeks. (ctb)

03/12/26- Confirmed with JOO that the new account numbers are set up and ready for bene change. Again, Donna is not listed anywhere on the trust so I am not sure if they have separate trust? I asked BES and he said to give him Tom a call but Tom answered and immediately hangup. I sent him MRC to give a call into the office. (ctb)

03/12/26- Tom called back in, they have one trust. Pulling now.

03/12/26- Pulled IRA paperwork for both tom and Donna to get the trust updated as contingent bene. Sent them both a docusign and MRC. I also noticed that Donna's Fidelity accounts have the wrong middle initial. The accounts show I when her middle initial is J. Opening a service request to try to get this changed. (ctb)

03/26/26- Tom stopped by the office for a meeting, I grabbed wet sigs and submitted to Fidelity.

- Your document(s) have been successfully submitted.
- Confirmation #: EDS202603260054193081

Submitted to WC, pushing back to follow up

03/05/2026 - CIDA ppwk pulled and emailed to KSO. She will print and snail mail tomorrow (baw)

03/10//2026 - Collin mailed 03/06 and I sent message today (baw)

Hello Tony,

I hope you’re doing well.

We have mailed a CIDA authorization form to you. This form will allow us to add your tax firm, Taxtyme, specifically Rebecca Ervin, so she can access the necessary information related to your account.

Once you receive the CIDA, please review, sign, and return it at your earliest convenience so we can proceed with granting Rebecca access. You may either mail the completed form back to us or drop it off at the office.

If you have any questions or need assistance, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

03/25/206 - Sent a MRC checking in to see if they received the CIDA ppwk in the mail (baw)

items.title: Beard, Brian - PENNMUTUAL SURRENDER
items.notes:
08/13/2025 - FJD review cover noted Cash Out Rachel PM WL and Leah's PM WL - send forms to Brian Beard. The statement is attached to this activity. I'm not familiar with Penn Mutual and not clear if both Brian and Andrea have separate policies? The statement that was given to me after the review meeting was Penn Mutual Policy 0082705460

Rachel's parents to cash out Penn Mutual whole life policies and distribute funds to their daughters (Leah and Rachel (ejd)

08/14/2025 - There are two separate policies - one for Leah & one for Rachel. I called and spoke with Sarah to verifiy surrender forms for their specific policies and PennMutual is telling me that **Frank needs his licensing updated and is currently not active**.  I was able to get information regarding both Leah and Rachel's policies.  I will send over the surrender forms to Brian, as he is the owner of them - we turned off the monthly auto premium payment - should they change their minds and keep the policies, APL will kick in after 60 days to keep policy from lapsing.  ALSO - they say it is a 15-18 day processing turnaround time. She also said that they will calculate cost basis and they MAY receive tax doc for reportable transaction.  And since they removed from auto pay, they will receive a quarterly bill via the mail, which they can disregard.

Completed forms can be emailed to lifeservicesrequests@pennmutual.com.

I cannot find the form on the site - reached out and requested the form that Sarah said I needed:

**Fixed Products**: Use Form PM6529FIXED (rev 01/25). (kso)

08/18/2025 - Sent paper forms to Brian via USPS regular mail. (kso)
